title: Cost of Capital: If the cost of equity is 0.1 and the expected...
summary: Calculate the cost of equity or expected return using the CAPM formula.
prompt: If the cost of equity is 10% and the expected growth rate is 5%, what is the implied dividend yield based on the Gordon Growth Model?
solution: Using the Gordon Growth Model, Cost of Equity = Dividend Yield + Growth Rate, so Dividend Yield = Cost of Equity - Growth Rate = 10% - 5% = 5%. This yield reflects the expected return from dividends relative to the current stock price.
